title: Carrying them with me
blurb: Grief didn't end — it changed shape, and so did life around it.
lead: A story about losing someone deeply loved, and slowly learning to live alongside the loss.

blocks.text: People kept talking about 'stages' and 'closure', as if grief were a task to complete. It wasn't. It came in waves — flattening at the strangest moments, receding, returning without warning.
For a while, the waves were constant. Getting through a day felt like an achievement. Well-meaning advice to 'move on' missed the point entirely — moving on felt like a betrayal.

What helped wasn't getting over it. It was small rituals — a photo, a favourite song, saying their name out loud. Letting the waves come instead of bracing against them. Letting other people help carry the weight.
The waves never fully stopped. But they came less often. And slowly, life grew back around the loss — not replacing it, but holding it alongside meaning and even joy again.
I didn't learn to let them go. I learned to carry them with me, and to keep living anyway.
This is an illustrative composite — a realistic story drawn from common experiences, not a real named individual. It's here to remind you that change is possible.
